Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order 1) Compelling Pltf Charles Woodrow To Submit To An Independent Psychological Examination 2) Compelling Pymt Of Cancellation Fee To Dr. Joanna Berg, And 3) Moving For Sanctions For Abuse Of The Discovery Process
Off Calendar Mar-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and PATSY THOMAS' Motion For Order 1) Compelling Plaintiff Charles Woodrow To Submit To An Independent Psychological Examination 2) Compelling Payment Of Cancellation Fee To Dr. Joanna Berg, And 3) Moving For Sanctions For Abuse Of The Discovery Process is granted in part and denied in part. CCSF's Motion to Compel Charles Woodruff to Submit to an Independent Psychological Examination is granted unless plaintiff stipulates in writing that he will not present an expert witness in support of his claim for negligent infliction of emotional distress. If plaintiff will not so stipulate, then the motion is granted subject to the parties first meeting and conferring in an effort to narrowly tailor the exam's scope. Mr. Woodruff's attorney may not be present at the exam absent the express written agreement of defendant or a showing of good cause to the court at the hearing of this motion as to why he should be allowed to be present.
The court does not have jurisdiction over Dr. Berg, but directs the City to ask her to invoice Mr. Woodruff and his attorney directly for her $900 cancellation fee less amounts, if any, by which she was able to mitigate lost income. If plaintiff and his attorney fail to pay the (mitigated) cancellation fee, and the City ends up paying it, then plaintiff and his attorney are ordered to reimburse the City for its actual out of pocket expense in this regard.
Sanctions are denied on the Motion to Compel. Even though plaintiff voluntarily appeared, however briefly, at the mental exam, CCP section 2032.310 mandates leave of court before obtaining such discovery. There was no prior leave of court here. No sanctions are awarded for abuse of the discovery process. However if plaintiff and his attorney fail to fully cooperate with the mental exam, then, given the evidence in the record here, sanctions will likely be awarded on further motion.
Counsel for defendant City and County of San Francisco is ordered to prepare proposed order for signature.
JUDGE PRO TEM THOMAS A. COHEN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
